conclusion: Layer3 full-attention produces under-tolerance but bit-different outputs in fullnative vs rowchunk2; layer4 RMSNorm then amplifies to over-atol. The actionable target is grouped full-attention bit-level composition/order or the sensitive handoff, not additional per-row pre-QKV diagnostics.

interpretation.fix_target: This points toward making grouped full-attention output bit-identical enough to rowchunk/c1 or handling the layer3->4 sensitivity; rowchunk grouping remains the exact control.
interpretation.layer3_origin: Layer3 fullnative and rowchunk2 are bit-different at captured pre-QKV/QKV stages but remain under tolerance through the layer3 output. The first over-atol c8 split still manifests after the layer3 output feeds layer4 linear-attention RMSNorm, not as an over-atol layer3 full-attention substage failure.

method: Same in-memory hidden-bisect capture as iter438, but comparing every captured layer3 full-attention substage between fullnative no-rowchunk and rowchunk2. No raw tensors stored.

prompt_verifier.evidence: PASS: focused direct layer3 full-attention substage comparator narrows the rowchunk/fullnative split to under-atol bit drift through layer3 output and confirms where over-atol begins; no performance claim.
